string.text: Task completed.
Great, which one?
The missing squad case.
Excellent, here's your reward.
Any work available?
Take a look at this:
The missing squad case.
I’ve changed my mind.
Sadly, there’s no work at the moment.
Alright, suit yourself.

One of our recon squads disappeared in the swamp area, we've lost contact. As much as I hate to admit it, they're probably dead. Go find them. If they’re dead, bring back their gear — we need the supplies, you understand. If they’re alive, help them return to base.
Alright, I’ll go look for your men.
I’m here to pick up the package.
Got it. Give me the money and you’ll get the goods.
Here you go.
Perfect, here’s the package.
Task completed.
Great, here’s your reward.
Greetings! Got any work?
There is. Need someone to act as a courier. Interested?
Sure, what’s the job?
Sorry, I changed my mind.
Unfortunately, there’s no work right now.
Alright, your call.

A friend of mine supplies me with medicine from Yantar. Sadly, he can’t make it to our base, so I need you to meet him instead. I’ll give you money to pay for the order. If there’s any change left, you can keep it. Just make sure to bring the case back here. I might even throw in some meds as a bonus — always handy, right?
Got it! I’ll be back soon.
Task completed.
Great, here’s your reward!
I need work — got anything?
Of course, there is!
I’m listening carefully.
You know what, I changed my mind.
There’s nothing available at the moment. Come back later.
Alright then.

Listen, I’m basically running the show here like a general, but the sergeant doesn’t let me go wild — you know, all for the good of the working people, Lenin and all that. So there’s this guy who brings me booze, tinctures, and other little goodies from Cordon and hides them here at the garbage heap. Could you bring them over? Don’t worry, I’ll make it worth your while. March that liver into action!
No problem — wait for me!
Oh, mercenary! Tell me, have you by any chance met a stalker named Lefty?
Yes, I did. But he’s no longer among the living...
That’s a shame... A real shame. Alright, did he have a PDA?
Hmm... That grief of yours doesn't seem very genuine... Something tells me you weren't really friends.
Merc, why would you even care?
Honestly, I don’t. So, what can you offer for his PDA?
AS Val and a couple of mags for it—deal?
Tempting offer!
Well? Do we have a deal?
Deal. Here you go!
I’ll think about it...
Yeger, is your offer still valid?
Of course, as agreed:\nAK-74 and a couple of mags.
[To himself: What a bastard...] Fine, take it.
[Assertive] Listen, we agreed on an AS Val and two mags, not an AK and rounds. Let’s settle this properly.
Pleasure doing business with you.
Listen here, “bluey,” that was *before* you refused to hand over the PDA. The offer’s changed. Don’t like it? Come back later and maybe I’ll throw in a sawn-off. [Laughs]
You bastard... Fine, take it...
There you are. So, did Gloomy bring you up to speed?
Sort of. But he said you'd tell me most of it.
Ready?
Are you really asking?
Then let’s move out!

Alright, the guys and I want to secure this territory and we need a base. The best option is an old factory, but mercs have holed up there. They’re not your friends, so no need to worry. The task is simple—take out their squads and occupy the factory. Got it?
The factory’s under our control. Here’s your promised payment and a personal gift from me.
If you’ve got more work, I’m in.
Glad to hear it!
Got any more work?
Look, like I said, we’re setting up a base here. As you’ve noticed, my squad is just a small part of the force. More squads are on the way, and we need supplies—rations, ammo, and medical kits.
Supplies are good and all, but where do I get them? Actually, never mind—I’ve got a place in mind. Just don’t feel like paying for it out of pocket. What’s the price?

Whoa, slow down! Hear me out—supplies and ammo need to be picked up from stalkers I’ve already made a deal with. It’s paid for, just pick it up. The whole thing’s worth maybe three to four thousand. As before—payment after delivery.
Alright then, wait for me.
Count me out this time.
Understood. What’s the payment?
Quick one, aren’t you? Seems like these documents mean a lot to you.
Spare me the drama. Just tell me what I need to do to get the documents.

Alright, no drama then. As you probably know, this area hasn’t been fully explored. Even local stalkers don’t know all the details. Turns out, there are powerful scanners in the old administrative building where the bandits used to be. Your task is to retrieve them.
And in return, I get the documents? Though I have a feeling it won’t be that simple.
You’re sharp. Here’s the deal: the documents are currently with Sapsan. I know you’re not on good terms, so I’ll send someone to retrieve them for me.
Now we’re talking! Alright, I’ll go look for those scanners.
Ten thousand. Payment on completion, don’t expect an advance or anything else.
Whew... Here are your scanners.
You look pale—what happened?
The scanners were guarded by Burers, the bastards! Lucky I reacted in time.
Yeah... Alright, I’ll give you a small bonus for that. And here are the documents. Hopefully, they’re what you were looking for.
Yes, yes, that’s them! Listen, can I ask you to use your contacts to find out if there are any more documents like this?
Why not. But in return, you’ll have to do a bit of work. Only if I find something, though—I’m not gonna send you running around for nothing.
Deal! Let me know if anything comes up—I'm heading back to camp.
Hmm... Alright, when do we move out?
Right now. We were just waiting on you.
Then let’s go!
Alright, let’s hear it—what’s the job?
Look, since your gear is “solid,” I won’t send you on anything too dangerous just yet. For starters, you need to find a stalker named Nekrasov.
Got it. Any leads on where to look?
Unfortunately, you’ll have to search the entire “Wastelands” area.
Alright... If I do find him, then what?
I’ve got a feeling he’s already dead, so bringing him back probably won’t happen. In that case, bring his PDA—we’ll see what’s what from there.
Alright, I hear you.
Mission accomplished! You were right—he’s dead. But it happened recently. Here’s his PDA.
Hmm... Looks like he was closely connected to stalkers. That’s good—we can use this to our advantage and maybe even improve relations a bit, thanks to this PDA. Keep it for now.
Care to fill me in on your plan? Because I’m not walking into a slaughter for no reason!

Easy now! Nobody’s sending you to die. You just need to take the PDA to the stalkers and hand it over. There’s nothing too sensitive on it, so the best option is to establish a connection with them. And it’ll help to have contacts with their “movement” leader. They’ve been here for half a year already, so they know the area and its inhabitants better than we do.
Alright, what about my reward—or was I chasing down that stalker for free?
There’s the Gryf I know... Here’s a small payout and some ammo for your gun.
Didn’t expect you to be so stingy. Still, better than a donut hole.
Alright, enough chit-chat! Head to the stalkers, and I’ll try to make sure they don’t shoot you on sight.
Fine, I’m on my way.
There you are! So, ready for another job?
Of course. Lay it on me.
Alright, you need to help some Freedom guys clear out an old factory. Talk payment with them yourself. I’d go myself, but I’m waiting on a messenger from Jackal.
Got it. Wait—what messenger?
I can’t share all the details, but I can tell you this—he can help us with finding those documents.
Nice! Alright, keep me posted.
Job’s done.
Good job! But something tells me you’re holding something back.
Yeah... This situation’s not great. But I still don’t get what we’re supposed to do next.
What’s next? Simple—we find the rest of the documentation on this project. Then we cash in and get the hell out of the Zone.

You’re saying reasonable things, but you're forgetting that we don’t have any intel: where to look, who might know the location. Though I think the stalkers here might be able to help — they’re not sitting around for nothing.
See? And you said I was forgetting something. No, my friend, I just chose not to mention the part you already know quite well.

So you're suggesting I go talk to the stalkers and start interrogating them? Like, "Hey man, you wouldn’t happen to know where to find some documents, would you? Lost them like 10 years ago and still can’t find them..." Is that it?
No! Ask subtly, casually. Believe me, if you ask the right questions while talking about something seemingly unrelated, you can get what you need.
Alright, I’ll give it a try. But first, you're going to tell me all the details about this job. I don’t like working blind.

Now that’s my student, haha. So, this job — we were hired by a professor from Yantar. I can’t give his name, he wanted to stay anonymous. He told us there’s a lab in the "Dead Lands" where the C-Consciousness conducted experiments to create mutants with psi abilities — basically, Controllers. He also said they kept detailed records and created an archive, which was split into several parts. How many exactly — he didn’t say, even though he probably knows. That’s all I got.
Interesting. Alright, time to go kiss up to some stalkers and get some intel.

Heh, you’re right! We found a PDA and some documents on the bandit. The documents are connected to Project "Prometheus". I already have part of them — just some scientist’s notes. But these new ones paint a much clearer picture.
Let me take a look.

Here you go. By the way, the latest messages found on the bandit's PDA show they were here specifically to look for those documents. But they only found a part of them. And who knows how many more parts there are...
Hmm... Damn! This is exactly what we’ve been looking for!
You serious?
More than serious. As for those bandits — we knew about them, that’s why we sent you to take them out. By the way, who were the messages from?
Some guy named "Mrakobes". But no specific info on who he is.
Goddammit! So that's the rat that's been screwing everything up! I thought the Zone was punishing us, but no — it’s just Jackal’s new favorite playing his own game.
Didn’t think the stalkers had such a tense relationship with the syndicate.
It wasn’t always like that. What you saw — that’s the result of what happened when we arrived here.
How many people are in the group? What’s the timeframe and the pay?
There are five of them, including the target. One day to complete the job. Payment is fifteen grand, plus a bonus.
I don’t understand what you’re talking about.
Alright, send me their coordinates.
What do you mean?

No deep meaning. When we arrived, we immediately decided to scout the area. We split up into pairs to cover more ground. That day, the fog was thick — couldn’t see a thing beyond arm’s reach. Anyway, I was with Siply — may he rest in peace — and we heard rustling in the bushes. Thought it was mutants and let off a few bursts. Turned out it was a stalker... After that, things went downhill fast. Now stalkers are out for blood.
And explaining yourselves wasn’t an option?!
They wouldn’t even listen.
I can try talking to them for you. I think stalkers aren’t fools — they’ll understand.
Maybe later. For now, I’ve got another job for you.
I’m listening.
There’s a bandit around here, goes by the name of Vint. You need to take him and his crew out.
Finally awake? Good! Now tell me — how the hell did you end up in the lab?

You’re making valid points, but you’re forgetting one thing—we have no information: Where to look? Who might know the location? Although, I think the stalkers around here could help. They’re not sitting here for nothing.
See? And you said I forgot. No, my friend, I just kept quiet about the part you already know well enough.

So, you’re suggesting I go talk to the stalkers and start interrogating them? Like, “Hey bro, you wouldn’t happen to know where to find some old documents, would you? Lost them about 10 years ago and still can’t find them…” That it?
No! You’ll be asking subtly, so to speak—casually in conversation. Trust me, if you ask the right questions while talking about something seemingly unrelated, you can get what you’re after.
Alright, I’ll give it a shot. But first, you’ll tell me all the details about this job. I don’t like working without knowing the full picture.

That’s my student! Heh-heh. So, a professor from Yantar hired us to find those documents. I can’t say his name—he chose to remain anonymous. He said there’s a lab in the “Dead Lands” where the C-Consciousness ran experiments creating mutants with psychic abilities—in other words, Controllers. He also mentioned they kept detailed logs and created some sort of archive, which they then split into parts. How many exactly? He didn’t say, though I’m sure he knows. That’s the situation.
Interesting. Alright, time to go kiss some stalker ass to get the info.

Heh-heh, you’re right! We found a PDA and some documents on the bandit. They’re related to Project “Prometheus.” I already had part of these documents, but it was just some scientist’s notes. These, though, give a much clearer picture.
Let me take a look.

Here you go. Also, from the last messages on the bandit’s PDA, it turns out they were here looking for those very documents—but they only found a part. And who the hell knows how many more parts there are...
Huh… Damn! This is what we’ve been looking for!
Are you serious?
Dead serious. As for those bandits—we already knew about them. That’s why we sent you—to take them out. By the way, who was the PDA message from?
Some guy named Mrakobes. No further info on who he is though.
Goddammit! So that’s the rat who’s been screwing us over! I thought the Zone was punishing us—but no, it’s Shakal’s new favorite playing his own game.
Didn’t expect the stalkers to be on such bad terms with the Syndicate.
Wasn’t always like this. What you saw is the aftermath of how we arrived here.
How many people in the group? Deadline and payment?
Five people, including him. Deadline is one day. Payment: 15 grand and a little something extra.
I don’t know what you’re talking about.
Alright, send me their coordinates.
What do you mean?

No point in meaning—when we got here, we immediately started scouting the area. We split up in pairs to cover more ground. That day was super foggy—couldn’t see past your own hands. Anyway, I was with Siply, may the Zone rest his soul, and we heard rustling in the bushes. Thought it was mutants, so we opened fire. Turns out it was a stalker... After that incident, stalkers have been on edge with us.
And explaining yourselves? That wasn’t an option?!
No one even wanted to hear us out.
I can try to explain things to them. I don’t think they’re stupid—they might understand.
Maybe later. For now, I’ve got another job for you.
I’m listening.
There’s a bandit here—goes by the name of Vint, if I’m not mistaken. We need him and his crew taken out.
Woke up, huh? That’s good. Now tell me—how’d you end up in the lab?
Alright, shall we move out?
You ready? Maybe you should check your gear again? Or finish your business?
No, I'm ready. Let's go...
Yeah, you're right! I'll be back soon...
What the hell?!...
Gloomy, where are all your men?!
I-I... don't know! They were supposed to wait for us here!
Damn it! Once again fate throws a wrench in our plans... Hmph... Alright, we have to keep moving. We didn’t waste all that effort searching for those damn documents for nothing!
You’re right, let’s move...
Alright, I’ll open the airlock.
Griff... I don’t get it... How?! They were professionals!
Gloomy, stop panicking! They were likely caught off guard. I only have one question...
Alright, I’ll tell you everything, just tell me — when did Jackal send you to this lab?
About three days ago... Why?

Hmph... Seems like he pulled something on you too. Alright, listen carefully and don’t interrupt! I’ll start from the beginning. First thing you need to know is — you’ve been in this lab for about a week. Next... Your group wasn’t randomly selected — you’ve all been on Jackal’s radar for a while. There just wasn’t enough proof that one of you was a mole. And in our line of work, we don’t take contracts on our own — you know how that ends. So Jackal decided to test you like this. In the end, we figured out the mole was someone named — Spider...
[Continue]
So, the point is — that Spider apparently found another key to the locked sector and took out our guys...
I don’t get it — how the hell could I have been stuck in this damn lab for almost a week?! No food, no water?! HOW?!
Sadly, I can’t answer that... I just don’t know...
Whatever... So what exactly are we supposed to find in this lab?!
Spider. Find and eliminate him.
Alright then, let’s move on.
Alright, alright, what’s the question?
How did their bodies end up in the sealed sector? We’re the only ones with a key card! You sure you're not hiding anything?
[Looks away, then glances back at Griff] Almost...
What do you mean *almost*?!
Do you remember how you even got into this lab? Why you were heading here in the first place?

Of course. I remember Jackal ordered me and a few trusted men to head down here and retrieve some documents. But as we descended, something went wrong... I found two from the group, but we never found the third...
Good that you remember that. You should also know — my group was sent by Jackal to look for yours. But we only found you. Sorry I didn’t tell you right away — had to be sure you weren’t the mole.
A mole? A search party?! I don’t get it...
What’s wrong with you?!
I feel... l-like crap... *cough* *cough*...
Damn it, I’ll go myself then!
Hello! I’ve got an unusual question for you.
Make it quick, I’m busy.
Alright! So, I’m looking for documentation on a certain project — it’s called “Prometheus.” I was hoping you might know where I can find the files or maybe even have them yourself.
Merc, even if I *did* have such files, I wouldn’t give them to you. As for information — no idea. That all?
Yeah...
I’d suggest you speak fast — unless you’re looking for a bullet.
I found a stalker, and he had a PDA. From the info inside, it’s clear you knew him.
A stalker? Knew him? PDA... You got any more details? Who are you talking about?
Here’s his PDA.
Nekrasov?! Damn... Wait, how do I know *you* didn’t kill him?
Do I look like a suicide bomber? If I killed him — or someone from my clan did — we wouldn’t have shown up here. This is a gesture of goodwill, nothing more.
Alright, I’ll believe you. But don’t get cocky — we’re not looking to cooperate with your kind.
Didn’t expect you to. Just let my guys buy some supplies and ammo from you now and then. Won’t do you any harm.
Fine — but you’re doing the shopping. If anyone else shows up at the base, they’ll get a one-way ticket to HELL. No line.
Got it, loud and clear.
Griff, what’s the word?
I’ve got two pieces of news for you.
Well? Were you able to get the guys out?
I was, but... the group came out of the “bubble” dead...
Damn... Ngh... Alright, you kept your end of the deal. Here’s the access card...
Thanks. Hang in there...
Gloomy said you wanted something from me?
Yes-yes! Griff, I urgently need help, I'm starting to lose hope...
Wait, can you explain clearly what happened? And what kind of help do you need from me?

Well, about 16 hours ago, our recon group got caught in a spatial bubble. I don’t think I need to explain to you what that is or how it works… Anyway, two of the guys are critical, and one of them is a very close friend of mine. I really don’t want to lose him… Damn, why did I send them out...
Pull yourself together! Look, I can try to pull your guys out of the anomaly, but in return I’ll need the keycard to the lab under the factory. Deal?
Alright, alright... I agree to everything! Just get them out!
Here’s your order.
Great! Listen, I’ve been thinking, and in addition to the payment I promised, I’ll throw in our uniform kit. I think you’ll appreciate it.
Pretty generous, thanks.
Hello, I’m here from Joker.
Yeah, yeah, here’s his order. Be careful with it, there are some fragile and quite dangerous items inside.
Got it...
What’s the talk about?
It’s awkward to ask you, you know how things are between our groups, but we have no other choice.
Spare me the sentimental crap. Just say what you need.
Basically, our trader has suspicions about one stalker, but they’re unfounded. It’s just talk, and without evidence, I can’t accuse him.
So I’m supposed to kill him?
NO! You just need to find proof of his involvement, and better yet, talk to Vasilisk – he’ll explain everything in more detail.
Alright, I’ll go talk to him.
Mercenary? Haven’t seen your kind in a while... Anyway, enough chit-chat! What do you want?
Found some interesting loot at the factory, just wondering who to give it to...
Loot, huh? Whose is it?
Beats me... Bandits hired me to find it and gave me the general area. No idea who it really belongs to.
Bandits?! Haha, well good thing you brought it to us! Hand it over, and here’s your reward for not giving it to those bastards – and for throwing a wrench in their plans!
Anytime. Let me know if you need anything else.
Need something fixed?
Merc, what do you want?
Did Sapsan explain what you’ll be doing?
Yes, though he left out some details. Better tell me everything from the beginning.

Alright, first lead is an abandoned village. Yeah, I know – lots of hiding spots. But thanks to Diesel, we managed to trace a signal from this "dandelion" guy, or rather, the spot he frequents the most. The second place is an anomalous swamp – the one with the downed MI-6 chopper. No exact location, so you’ll need to search the whole swamp and nearby area.
Hmm… Alright, I’ll check it out. If I find anything, I’ll contact you.
You won’t be able to reach me – can’t say why right now. So you’ll have to report back in person.
Alright...

Okay, listen up. A new guy recently arrived at the camp – looks like a harmless softie, but he's actually a real bastard... He’s been telling all sorts of tales, and not just made-up stories – he’s taking credit for other people’s accomplishments.
So what? He’s just telling stories, isn’t he? Or is there more to it?
There’s more, though it’s all just guesses for now… But we can verify them.
Stop dragging it out already...
Word is, this "dandelion" guy was getting close to stalkers who later either vanished or were found half-dead… But we haven’t been able to prove anything yet!
If I understood correctly, you want me to find evidence. Right?
Exactly! I already have a couple of leads on where the evidence might be. No idea how reliable they are, but it’s worth checking. If you find anything – bring it straight to me!
Alright, give me your leads, I’ll check them out.
Look what I managed to find.
Let’s see… Damn! It’s locked, although… I’ll hand it over to Diesel, he’ll extract the data.
What should I do?
For now, go get some rest. As soon as I find out what’s on the flash drive, I’ll let you know.
Alright, take care…
What’s the urgency?
Remember that flash drive you brought me?
Yeah, but it was locked...
It *was*! But Diesel managed to extract all the data. Based on what we found, I was able to prove that this "dandelion" was involved in those stalkers’ deaths.
That’s great! Glad I could help.
Anyway, Sapsan ordered a reward to be given to you — here, take it!
Much appreciated.
Hey, I need your help!
What kind of help?
Basically, I found a pretty valuable artifact the other day, but I couldn’t get to it because of mutants.
And what do you need from me?
I just need you to watch my back, that’s all. Deal? We’ll split the profit fifty-fifty.
Sounds pretty good… Alright, fine, let’s go!
Let’s move.
Well, look who dropped by! Honestly didn’t expect to see you here. So, what brings you around?
Just thought I’d take a “vacation,” but seems like it’s not going too well...
Got it. Well then, let’s get to the point. Why are you here?

Long story short — Sapsan, who’s temporarily filling in for the scout leader, sent me here. He’s concerned about a recent incident where your men killed one of his scouts. Naturally, he wants to know why it happened, and also what your purpose is in this area.
Yeah, I remember that. But you see, it was an unfortunate accident. As for why we’re here — you’re a mercenary too, you know how confidential this kind of stuff is.
I get it, I get it. Then at least tell me what exactly happened.

Sure. We came in at night. Everything was quiet, calm… but there was such thick fog that you couldn’t see your own hand. Then we heard rustling in the bushes — figured it was a mutant! So, we let off a couple bursts. That’s how it went...
Interesting story... Alright, I’ll talk to the stalkers and explain everything. I’m sure they’ll understand — they’re not idiots.
That’d be nice.
Alright, I’m off.
Why so quiet?
Speak.
I need to deliver some money to someone.
That’s it? You’re being secretive, just say it.
Alright… The stalker who agreed to deliver the documents — he’s got a sister not far from here. You just need to deliver what I promised him. That’s all…
Alright, I’ll take care of it. No need for payment.
You sure?
Yeah. Give me the money and I’ll be on my way.
Here you go, and thank you.
[With a smile] Oh, come on… I’ll owe you one.
Yes, I’m thinking about it...
What are you thinking about?
How to properly say that I completed your task.
Ugh! I don’t even know what to think anymore. Just give me the documents.

Here you go. By the way, I got lucky! I didn’t have to run around looking for the documents. It turned out that the stalker had already retrieved them from the stash and was keeping them, probably thinking of selling them. But, as always, we arrived just in time!
Well done, here’s your reward.
Thank you very much. Is there any other work?
There’s something, but the pay is small.
You… Who are you?
Not so important, what matters is that I was asked to give this to you [Give PDA and Money].
Can I come with you?
Why not, let’s go.
Unfortunately, no. Sorry.
What is this and who asked you to deliver it?
This is the PDA and money that your brother earned, but sadly couldn’t take himself. Who gave it to me is not important.
He… What?!...
I’m sorry...
[Crying].
[Through tears] Where are you going?
To the Big Land.
Man... Cough... Cough... Quickly... Over here...
Oh, what a surprise! And no bodyguards? Strange, I thought your group wouldn't leave you behind, or did something change?
I don’t know, really don’t know. They just told me to kidnap and deliver him. That’s all I know.
Fine, I’ll believe you. Now give me the medallion.
Well, you can call me sentimental, but that’s who I am. About a week ago, my stash got robbed. Of course, I dealt with the thief, but I still don’t know where he hid what he stole.
What exactly did the thief steal?
And because of some knife, you want me to turn "The Dump" upside down?
This isn’t just any knife, it’s my grandfather’s knife. He kept it from World War II... This knife is very precious to me, as a memory.
Alright, I’ll help you find it! Do you have any clues?
Here, take this. It’s the PDA of that stalker, but there’s some nonsense written on it! Maybe you can make sense of it.
Sorry, but I can't just give it away. But I can make a deal with you, and if you do everything as needed, I’ll return the medallion.
Let me take a look. If I find it, I'll let you know immediately.
Either you give everything now and stay alive, or...
Well, whatever...
[Shoot]
.....Give... Me... A... First Aid... FIRST AID...
Fine, here. But don't try anything funny, or you'll see hell before you even think about anything.
Thanks, man...
Don't get too cocky, listen carefully and answer the questions. If you answer correctly, you’ll stay alive!
Got it, ask away...
What about the Wild's group?
Why did they abandon you?
Where's the medallion you stole?
It's with me.
I don’t know. When I got what I was ordered to get, I reached the meeting point, but there was an ambush waiting for me. A sniper shot me from that tower, and then just disappeared...

It’s a squad I was part of or used to be part of... It's hard to say now. The squad was formed around the commander, a former military man who deserted after an incident at Agroprom when mercenaries stormed the complex. After that, he formed a group of the most experienced stalkers, with the goal of taking control of the Zone... I know, his tactics are a bit off, but believe me, he controls half of the Duty faction now, and with that, their territories.
Why do they need that medallion?
Well, here you are! Listen, we haven’t introduced ourselves yet. I think we should fix that, don’t you think?
No objections! I’m Grif, and you are?

I’m called Sapsan, I’m something like the leader here, but it’s mostly a formality. The real leader went on a raid a couple of weeks ago, and we haven’t heard from him since, so for now, I’m in charge. I think we can get down to business now, if you’re okay with that.
Let’s do it.

Alright, listen up. Just a few days ago, a squad of mercenaries arrived on our controlled territory. You might think, so what? But it’s not that simple. They killed one of my scouts. What bothers me is that we supposedly have a truce with them. In other words, they don’t touch us, and we don’t touch them. We don’t interfere in each other's business.
Okay, that’s interesting! But I still don’t get what you need from me?
So, your task is to find out why they killed my scout, and if possible, figure out what the mercenaries need in this area. Got it?

I got it, but here are a couple of details. The Syndicate in the Zone might be one, but it has many leaders. In other words, I don’t know which group is involved, and if they’re enemies. Not all mercenaries get along, some groups just do jobs, while others started their own activities. Also, I’ll need some gear, because mine isn’t in the best condition...
Got it, there’s always a risk. As for the gear, go see our tech, Diesel. He’ll give you a standard set of gear. Good luck.
Alright. I’ll go redeem my “Past Sins”...
Well, here you are again. So, did you find out anything?
Found out! So, listen up [talk about the incident.]

Ugh... A headache... Well, it's better than a war with the mercenaries. If I’m being honest, we probably made the mistake of sending our guy on the recon. The important thing is that it’s all resolved now, and the rest doesn’t matter. By the way, did they tell you the purpose of their visit?

No, and they won’t. This is Syndicate business, and it’s better not to get involved. Tell your people to stay at least 200-300 meters away from the church, the guys there are on edge and might shoot. Also, here’s a tip for the future: never, under any circumstances, get involved with the mercenaries’ affairs! The consequences can be pretty bad. Understand, they’re hired for serious jobs, and if they fail, they become corpses themselves. That’s why they act together and are always on edge. I hope you got that.

Got it, and I’ll pass along your advice about the church to my men. By the way, while you were out, someone named Tikhiy arrived at the camp. He wanted to talk to you. You should meet with him. Also, here’s some money for your trouble. If you need ammo or gear, there’s a safe over there [points to the safe near the door].
Alright, thanks. I’ll go talk to this Tikhiy.
Hello, Diesel.
Hello, hello! Are you here on business or just hanging out?
On business. I won’t beat around the bush! I need help unlocking a PDA, can you help me out?
Sure, no problem. Let me see it!
Here you go.
So, look. I won't take any money from you, and I don't have any tasks for you either. However, you'll have to wait for a while because I have some things to take care of myself.
Whoa, unexpected! Thanks, I'll wait as long as needed.
So, what's the verdict?
It's all ready, here you go.
Thanks a lot, if you need anything – I’m at your service.
Haha, well, nobody dragged it out of you.
Alright, I'm off.
Hello, foreign guest. Sapsan told me you needed something, here you go, and be careful with it, or you'll have to return it! [Smiles]
Return it?

Relax... Haha, you fell for it. You don't have to return anything. This is, so to speak, a gift or an advance. Anyway, I won’t bore you with boring talk, here take something else from me, I think it'll come in handy.
Thanks for the Kalash! Alright, I think it's time to go. Bye, and thanks for the Kalash!
Well, you can jump for joy now.
I was starting to doubt, but you pleasantly surprised me! Here’s your reward and give me my medallion.
However, it’s sad...
Listen, you’re asking about all this and listening... I understand that you also suffer?
One could say that.
Would you tell me about it?

Well, I can. There’s nothing secret here. A couple of years ago, I was just a simple emergency worker, working, eating, drinking, and not thinking about anything. But one day, my little sister was hit by a car. You know, if the driver was at fault, I wouldn’t blame myself so much. Unfortunately, it all happened very prosaically, she was just walking near her school on the playground, and this scum decided to take a shortcut... You see, I’ve long realized what happened and that nothing can be undone, but I feel more sorry for my mother, who lost her little angel... [A tear rolled down from Grif’s eye.]
Sorry for making you remember that again.
You know, I’m grateful to you. I’ve been doing this “job” for two years. I’ve never told anyone about this.
Grif, listen! You need to leave here. You won't achieve anything here, and the pain, it will eventually subside, trust me, I know. You have a mother, do yourself and her a favor, leave...
Sorry, but my journey is not finished yet.
You’re right, I’ll leave...
Here you go and listen, I examined the medallion, and I don’t understand why you’re paying me ten thousand for some damn medallion?!
You know, I’m not even surprised you’re asking. You’re a mercenary, your nerves are like steel, and you can't understand what sentimental memories are...
I may be a mercenary, but I’m also a human, and yeah, maybe I overdid it with my temper in some moments, but that’s life, if it’s not you, then it’s someone else. So, will you tell me or not?

This is a family heirloom, this medallion has been passed down for more than 300 years... The last person who gave it to me was my father, and his father gave it to him. My father lived here in the Chernobyl Exclusion Zone back in '86, I was a kid back then, but he told me: "You must be strong, even if everyone turns their back on you! You must find new paths, and remember, you create your own destiny!" Since then, this medallion has been very dear to me.
What happened to your father?
He died... He was among those who perished in the explosion. You know, I remember that night, but I can’t recall my father’s face...
Don’t you have any photos?
Everything was left in Pripyat... Although, I’ve already been there, but the apartment was completely cleaned out and not a single photo, I didn’t find anything.
Well, hello. I was told you were looking for me.
You must be Grif, well, nice to meet you!
Alright, I’ll try to find your "Medallion".
To hell with it! Find your "Medallion" yourself. I don't care about searching radioactive wastelands for some scout who stole your "Medallion". Goodbye.
Yes, it's me. But you know, time is money, so get to the point quickly, or you'll end up on the list of the departed.
Easy, easy! I need help with something.
Didn't I make myself clear? I said quickly! I don't have time for your riddles.

Got it! Listen up: the other day, my group and I were wandering the outskirts, and by chance, we ran into a pack of snorks, and in the chaos, I lost my Medallion somewhere. I would have gone and gotten it myself, but the problem is, one of the group members quickly picked it up and ran off with it.
What exactly do you want me to do? And what's the payment?

You need to find this stalker and take my Medallion from him. His name is Petruha. He's a decent guy, but something must have made him do this. Whether you kill him or not is up to you, but bring me the Medallion. I'll pay you 5000 rubles.
Tempting offer! But now clarify where I can find him?
Ah, that's the part I don't know. The only thing that's certain is that Petruha hasn't left the "Dump" area and is still hanging around somewhere nearby.
I found his PDA, and there was... a rather interesting note! It instructed the finder to pass the documents to you. (Lie.)
Where are you rushing off to so quickly? What did you want to talk to me about?
Where am I rushing to?... [Inhales and explains about the meeting with Sokol and what he revealed.]
Grif, if I leave here, I will only leave with you. It's your choice...
You're not as simple as you might seem... Alright, let's go.
You said he's dead... How could you talk to him?!
It's hard to explain, and I don't fully understand it myself... But as Sokol said, he thinks it's related to the time and place of his death. To put it simply, I spoke with a ghost...
No-no... This can't be!!! You're lying!
If I were lying, how would I know you're his sister? By the way, there was no mention of this in his PDA...
[Silent]

By the way, about the conversation I mentioned... Listen, you need to leave here, I would even say run! There's nothing good here, this is a purgatory, where "demons" of all kinds have gathered. What you see as kindness or sympathy in someone is just the survival instinct, which is essential...

Instinct? And what about your actions?! You mean to say that they will help you survive? This is nonsense that you've made up for yourself! The "demons" you're talking about are only in your head and nowhere else!...
[Smiling] Well, maybe...
Grif, where have you been?
[Angrily] It doesn't matter, what do you want?
Why are you like this?!
Sigh… Sorry, just a lot has happened in the last day, and I really don't feel like talking about it.
Alright, I won't torture you, I'll just ask. Did you manage to find Sokol?

Found him... But unfortunately, he's dead. I managed to find his PDA and get information about who killed him, but the place where the killer might be, I have no idea, some abandoned camp... Maybe you know where it is? And here, take his PDA and some extra stuff...
Wait! The PDA, I get, but what's in the case? What's inside?
In the case, there’s money. Sokol wanted to pass it to you, but he couldn't, so I decided to help him out. Alright, let's talk about it later, but first, do you know where this abandoned camp is?

Uhm... Alright... An abandoned camp, you say? Well, there's one not far from here, hunters used to stop there for a break, but something happened, and they abandoned it. The camp is to the southwest, near the swamp.
Thanks, I’ll be back soon, and we’ll need to talk, just wait for me!
Hello again! Sapsan said I could get a special kit from you.
Yes-yes, of course! Here you go.
Thank you.
Listen, be careful, those guys are serious. I checked them out through my channels, they are an elite squad of some Dark One. They say he's a real tough guy.
I'll manage somehow... And it’s probably just a setup...
Grif, I wouldn’t be so sure… I got the info from mercenaries, if you know Cap, you’ll understand that the information is reliable.
They’re just bandits, I’m used to difficulties...
Well, good luck to you, come back alive or not at all...
What’s the emergency?!
I’ll explain, but first tell me, did you take care of the squad?
I took care of them. I found one of the bandits’ PDA, and it has some very interesting correspondence, but here's the problem, half of the messages are erased. I don’t think I can recover it.

Dammit... Alright, give me that PDA and listen. While you were dealing with the squad, the Jackal himself contacted me and claimed that we were violating the contract. I, of course, disagreed. But he wouldn’t listen. Anyway, mercenary squads will be arriving soon, and we only have one option.
What’s that?
Run...
You're changing your opinion fast, but I won’t judge. I’m not going anywhere, I’m a merc myself and I’m not afraid that my own will come. Besides, this was a job, nothing personal.
You’re right, it’s not your place to judge us! But anyway, thanks for the help. It was nice working with you, good luck, Grif...
Hello, Grif. Good to see you! Listen, have you seen Yegor by any chance?
I’ve seen him, and I even know where he is right now...
Where is he?
That bastard is lying near the swamp.
Is he dead? Who did it?!
Don’t act dumb! You know perfectly well.
But I still don’t understand, why?
[Explain what happened and hand over the documents].
Well… I didn’t think Yegor would be such a bastard... Alright, thanks, and here, take this from me personally, you’ve earned it.
Grif, sorry for interrupting you! But there’s a good reason for it.
Alright... Speak up.
Just now, a guy named Frown contacted me, and he said that his people intercepted bandit communications. Based on that, they plan to capture our camp.
That Frown... Wait, what do you want from me?
We need someone to eliminate their strike squad, that will at least give us some time to prepare.
Great, I was just about to retire, and now I’m being turned into cannon fodder. Well done, Sapsan!
Stop joking! Grif, understand this! If you don’t do this now, tomorrow we won’t be here, and no, we won’t leave. We’ll just die here.
Dammit... [Remembers the letter from the girl.] Fine, I’ll help, give me the coordinates of those “devils”...
Thank you, Grif. Here are the coordinates, and also, go to Diesel, he’ll give you a special gear set, you’ll need it.
Alright, I’m going.
I told you, it's not important!
Why are you attached to this place?
Exactly, I can't answer that question because I don't know myself, but I suspect the murder is connected to the place and time of my death.
Alright, I think that's enough. Let's get to the point.

Okay, listen, tell Yulia that you found my body, and give her my PDA. Here it is, just in case. It has the coordinates of a stash with money and some documents. Give the money to Yulia, and give the documents to Yegor, then watch his reaction. Also, don't forget to mention me and say I personally handed them to you! Got it?
Okay, I'll do everything. Can you please tell me your real name? Don't ask why, you won't understand why I need it. Just tell me.
Okay, I'll do everything. Can you please tell me your real name? Don't ask why, you won't understand why I need it. Just tell me.
Thank you for your honesty! I won't let you down!
Oh! You're awake, well, that's good. Looks like you really got yourself in trouble!
Got myself in trouble? Hm... I don't think so.
So here's the deal! Either you help us and make a little profit, or you get a bullet in your head and we find another fool...
[Lie] Fine! Tell me, what's the deal?
You must be an idiot... If you're trying to corner me, I'll take you all down in an instant.
Shit... Fine, get out of here!
Goodbye...

So, recently my buddy passed away and left something valuable at the factory, he didn't say exactly where, but it's known that the stash is real. Bring it to us, and I'll give you some cash and let you go.
Sounds pretty interesting! Alright, I'll find your buddy's stash...
That's good! The marker is already in your PDA, and if you need anything, we're always here, and Secretary Cherep will welcome you anytime!
Uh-huh...
Brave? Yeah?! Well, we'll fix that...
Heh... You guys are funny, alright enough talk! Tell me what you need and scatter...
Well, pray, goat!
Already started!
What do you need? Well, for example, I really liked your gear, I've wanted something like it for a long time! Don't worry... We won't take everything, we're not animals.
Gear? Are you out of your mind? Should I also give you the keys to my apartment and meet you at the doorstep with some bread?

Oh... Looks like the guy doesn't understand humor, well, whatever. Anyway, my guys checked you out, and it turns out you're a merc, and what a merc! It's strange though, you're wandering these Godforsaken lands alone... Don't you want to share anything?
Share? No.
Alright, I won't waste time trying to convince you, I'll just put you in a tough spot.
Hello, stalker. What do you need?
I'm looking for a stalker named Sokol, do you happen to know where I can find him?
Sorry, no... I wasn't even acquainted with him.
Alright, sorry for disturbing you.
Oh, hello! How can I help?
Do you happen to know where I can find a stalker named Sokol?
Unfortunately, I rarely leave this room, the outside world is becoming an even bigger mystery to me every day...
Eh... Alright, sorry for bothering you.
So, what interesting news do you have?
Me? Well, I can say that I completed my task, and those stalkers are already free, and they'll be here soon.
Seriously?!
Yes! Now it's your turn to share some information.

Alright. The last time Sokol was seen near an abandoned church, but what was strange is that he was just standing there looking at the sky. It wasn't just looking… We called out to him, and he didn't respond... We stood there for a couple of minutes and then left, afraid to approach, thinking maybe the controller had taken over his mind. So, you should look for him there.
Got it, hope this is true information. Goodbye.
Hello, mercenary! What do you need?
Well, I won't beat around the bush... I need to know where stalker Sokol might be right now.
Hmm... I can tell you, but in return, I ask for help. Agreed?
Couldn't be otherwise, so tell me what I need to do to get the information.

Alright, listen up. Recently, one of my people intercepted a radio message from some stalkers. From the message, it was clear that they got trapped in a spatial bubble. Your task is to figure out how to get them out of there. Got it?
Clear as day. So, I help you, and you help me. But tell me, is the information about Sokol worth it? And is it even true?
Don't worry! It's all honest, you help me, and I help you.
Alright, give me the coordinates where "your guy" intercepted that message.
I don't know if it will help you or not, but here you go. I hope it works out for you.
Ah... You're not the only one hoping for that...
Hello, mercenary! Need something?
Well, I have a little job.
I'm listening.
In general, I was hired to search for a stalker named Sokol, and they said he was recently seen here. As the leader of this camp, I think you might know something about him.
Yes, there was such a guy, but I personally didn't talk to him because I was quite busy. If my memory serves me right, ask Eger, he must have talked to him.
Okay, thanks!
You took your time, but never mind. Let's get to the point, if you don't mind.
Alright, let's go.
Don't worry, this won't affect the job.
Okay, who exactly am I looking for?
The stalker, named Sokol, was last seen near the stalkers' camp. So, I think it makes sense to ask around there.
Alright, I got it.
What do you want as a reward for finding him?
Information.
What kind of information?
You’ll find out later. For now, I’m going to find "your stalker"...
I saw how you dealt with those bandits, and based on the way you did it, I can say you're a professional.
What are you getting at?
I need help, and I think you're the one who can handle it.
What exactly do I have to do?
You need to find one stalker.
Is that all?
Yes.
I think you're not telling me something...
Dizel, they say you have any kind of radio equipment.
Well... Not everything, but I do have some stuff, why?
I need an old army frequency scanner, a simple RFID. Don't tell me you don't have it...
I've got one, but why should I just give it to you?
How much do you want?
Five thousand, and that's the price.
Here, take it, give me the scanner.
I don't have that much money yet.
Here you go, still warm! Heh-heh.
When you have the money, come back!
Thanks...
Here I am again, but this time with the piece of junk.
Broke it? It happens... No returns or exchanges on this product!
Hmm... I thought Sid, the miser, had no competition, but turns out he does! Alright, enough idle chatter. I'm here for business, I need to extract and decode the data from this scanner.
Ah, even like that! Alright, we'll do it. You know my fee, but this time as a regular client, I'll let you pay only after the extraction and decoding!
How generous of you! Alright, it's a deal!
Great! Give me the scanner and go ahead, I'll contact you once it's ready.
Got it, I'm waiting.
I got your message, what's going on?

Everything's like this morning, after a couple of shots! Easy and casual, in short, I extracted and decoded the info, it's 2500 from you, and we'll be square. As a bonus, I'll give you a special detector that will help you break the "Bubble".
Deal!

The coordinates for exiting the anomaly, I sent them to your PDA. Once you're in the sending zone, the program will work for you, all you need to do is turn on the detector, and it will emit a directed pulse to break the "Bubble".
Thanks, take care.
Gloomy, the job's done.
I wasn't doubting you, Grif, really glad you got my guys out of that nasty "surprise." What kind of creature was it?
Well, it was a bloodsucker, kind of unusual though. It waited until I got closer and attacked, it turned out to be an interesting fight...
Interesting, interesting, you've earned your reward - here you go, hard-earned!
Got any work?
Got something.
I'm all ears.

So, look, recently my fighters were returning from a mission and ran into a bloodsucker. It wouldn't be a problem, but the creature was tough. My guys fired a few mags into it, and it didn't even flinch! Long story short, almost the whole squad is dead, but one managed to survive and gave the coordinates of the attack location. I think the creature is still there. Will you take the job?
I'll take it, what's the payment?
No-no-no, you do it yourselves.
Well, fine, if you don't want to earn, don't.
Three thousand, I won't give more.
Alright, wait here.
Got anything interesting to share?
Unfortunately, I have nothing interesting to tell, I haven't been anywhere for a long time...

You say interesting?... Well, there was this one story, so some Freedom guys came here, led by a Miklukh, wandered around the area, asking locals if they'd seen any soldiers. There were no soldiers here, honestly, although when the helicopter crashed, one survived, but he didn't stay long, he left pretty quickly... Rumors say he hid some loot here, but no one knows exactly where. He was often seen near the swamp, and there's a hole leading to some rooms, but no one goes there...
Doesn't seem that interesting...

Yeah, there was this one story. So, there were two friends - Sokil and Yegor. Sokil decided to make some extra cash and told his friend about it. Instead of helping, Yegor refused, but then, after Sokil had done all the work and was ready to turn it in, Yegor shot him... Rumor has it someone dealt with Yegor too. I think they did the right thing, no need for such scum wandering around the Zone...

Well, I was walking... mm... anyway, I was told in one hut there's a cool place. Cool... Artifacts, like, a stash of someone's. I get there, knock on the door... it's locked... What's this? Knock again - still locked... So I open it, and... it closes itself... I realized someone's not letting me in... Well, I climb through the window. Inside, I catch my breath, straighten up, and go to check who's messing with the door... A big room... a table in the middle... long like a highway... and a chair beside it, tall like a tower. Then someone whispers in my head (unintelligible)...

Well, listen. About a month ago, me and a couple of my buddies were walking through the Dark Valley, we stumbled upon some camp, which shouldn't have been there, because we passed by forty minutes ago and there was no camp, and the trail wasn't great either. So we approach and see a fire burning, new tents, not a hole in them, and no one around! We thought, maybe they went for firewood or something. Anyway, we decided to wait, sat by the fire, and in five minutes we passed out, though we weren’t even sleepy. We wake up, and there's a stalker in a merc suit standing next to us saying: "Hm... Another group of travelers, well, welcome...". We ask: "Where to?". He stays silent... We look around, and there’s nothing, no trees, no bushes, not even grass! I don’t know what it was, but my friend Kolyan said that ghosts were trying to communicate with us. I don’t even remember how we left... Since then, I don’t trust strange camps, who knows what's waiting there...
Tell us about yourself.

Well, what to tell? I used to be just a stalker, started like all the new guys at the Cordon, running errands for Sidorovich and Wolf, then slowly got on my feet and decided to go deeper into the Zone. I ended up in the "Hell" – if I can put it that way, then I got wounded and was found near a checkpoint at the Bar. How I got there, I don’t know… I don’t remember! But I didn’t stay long at the Bar, I met Diesel and Sapsan there. After I fully recovered, we went to Yantar, worked for the Scientists, doing small jobs for which we didn’t get much pay, but the last order from the Scientists was the best one, it was an easy task and we finished it quickly, got the promised reward, then packed up and went off into uncharted territory... And now we’re here...
Tell us about yourself.

I haven’t been asked such questions for a long time... Well, I’ll tell you. I’m not bothered. So, I’m a former military engineer. I arrived in the Zone among the first. Back then, even the 'Cordons' weren’t fully mapped. At that time, when I was still with the military, me and a few others were sent on a reconnaissance mission. So we’re heading out, and with us was a lieutenant, a young guy, obviously a conscript, who they decided to send as cannon fodder. We reached a village, and we saw a fire burning! Well, we thought maybe one of the villagers stayed behind and didn’t comply with the evacuation order. We went to check, and as we approached the fire, we saw a stalker sitting near it. At that time, we didn’t know he was an illegal, but he jumped up, aimed his double-barrel at us, and yelled, 'You bastards, never went beyond the checkpoint! What the hell died in the forest?!' The lieutenant, out of fear, fired a couple of bursts, the stalker died, and the lieutenant got promoted and given a vacation, saying he eliminated an especially dangerous violator... You see, after that moment, I started noticing that my commanders were engaging in rather secretive activities, strange orders, and a shortage of weapons and ammo, even though we had just received supplies from the mainland. So, I decided to stick my nose in and find out what was going on. I confronted the commander, asking what was going on and where the gear was. He calmly replied: 'If you keep opening your mouth where it’s not needed, you’ll end up like that stalker, lying next to a radioactive pile.' After that, I gathered everything I had and moved on. Along the way, I met Sapsan, and thanks to him, I’ve gained significant trust among stalkers and other factions now.
However, an interesting story.
Tell us about yourself.

About myself? What’s there to tell?... I’m just a medic, I used to work for the government. I came to the Zone with a scientific expedition, but due to disagreements and a series of failures, I decided to leave. I worked for a long time with the 'Duty' faction, but then I found out that stalkers were gathering a team to raid new territories, and they didn’t have a medic, so I decided to join them.
Tell us about yourself.
Is there nothing else for you to do? Sorry, but my life is my personal story, and I don’t feel like sharing it with everyone.
I understand, sorry.
I heard you might offer a contract for artifact hunting?

Well, a contract is a bit of an exaggeration, it’s more of an assignment for a search and payment, that’s all. Although, it depends on how you look at it. Artifacts are needed for creating some medicinal products and for newer, more advanced equipment, something like that.
Got it, what kind of artifacts do you need?
At the moment, I need an artifact called "Moonlight," I’ll pay good money for it, so take it, you won’t regret it!
I’ll find "Moonlight."
I’ve changed my mind.
Task completed.
Great! Give me the artifact and get your well-earned reward!
Here, take it.
Excellent! Here’s your reward.
Excellent! Here’s your reward.
Excellent! Here’s your reward.
Excellent! Here’s your reward.
